full_prompt: Design a single 4-axis crop-row weeding arm end-effector for a field robot operating in tomato rows on California raised beds. The end-effector mounts to a mobile platform driving at 0.50 m/s, reaches across a 0.76 m row spacing, and removes weeds between crops planted on 0.30 m in-row spacing without damaging crop stems of 8 mm diameter. The mechanism is a stainless steel articulated arm with links 250 mm + 250 mm + 150 mm, carrying a distal oscillating blade tool of mass 0.6 kg that strikes the soil with a vertical motion up to 40 mm amplitude at up to 5 Hz. Drive motors are NEMA-17 frameless servos at each joint with continuous torque up to 2.0 N-m each. The arm must simultaneously satisfy: (R1) stainless steel link maximum von Mises stress under the worst-case inertia plus soil reaction load must not exceed 125 MPa (Type 304 yield 205 MPa divided by SF 1.65), (R2) end-effector tip positioning error with 0.6 kg payload at 5 Hz oscillation must not exceed 5 mm at the blade tip, (R3) total end-effector mass including blade and links must not exceed 3.0 kg to protect the platform payload budget, (R4) a linear buckling eigenvalue check on the longest link under a 50 N axial accidental strike must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 3.0, and (R5) the first natural frequency of the assembled arm must be at least 15 Hz which is 3x the maximum 5 Hz operating forcing frequency to avoid resonance. Vibration (modal) analysis is required for R5. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with each link and joint as separate sub-bodies plus a motion and structural report listing max von Mises, tip positioning error, total mass, first buckling load factor, and first natural frequency.
short_prompt:
Design a 4-axis stainless-steel crop-row weeding arm end-effector for a field robot in a tomato row. The arm mounts to a platform driving at 0.5 m/s across 0.76 m row spacing, with links approximately 250 + 250 + 150 mm and a distal 0.6 kg oscillating blade (up to 40 mm vertical stroke at up to 5 Hz). Each joint is a NEMA-17 frameless servo (continuous torque up to 2.0 N-m).

Base is rigidly mounted to the mobile platform. Load cases.
- LC1 worst inertia + soil reaction: 80 N soil reaction at the blade tip at 5 Hz peak (blade acceleration ~40 m/s^2).
- LC2 accidental strike: 50 N axial impulse along the longest link.
- LC3 modal: require the first natural frequency above the 5 Hz forcing with margin.

End-effector total mass is a mobile-platform payload-budget constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Joint-level inertia and reaction forces are solved via rigid multibody dynamics, then fed to FEA per link.

prompt.title: 4-axis agricultural weeding arm end-effector
prompt.brief: Design a 4-axis stainless steel weeding arm (links 250+250+150 mm, 0.6 kg blade at 5 Hz) for 0.76 m tomato rows at 0.50 m/s travel, passing Type 304 stress, positioning, mass, buckling, and 15 Hz resonance floor.

material.links: Type 304 stainless steel, yield 205 MPa, E 193 GPa, density 8000 kg/m3

load_cases.name: worst_inertia_plus_soil_reaction
load_cases.application: distal blade tip at 5 Hz peak

objective.primary: pass stress, positioning, and resonance limits

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam for links, solid for joints

eval_coverage: resonance floor relative to forcing frequency
